2017-11-26.title: In Peru’s Deserts, Melting Glaciers Are a Godsend (Until They’re Gone)
2017-11-26.abstract: Accelerating glacial melt in the Andes caused by climate change has set off a gold rush downstream, letting the desert bloom. But as the ice vanishes, the vast farms below may do the same.

2017-11-26.title: As Anti-Semitism Rises, ‘I Don’t Feel Safe in Australia,’ Rabbi Says
2017-11-26.abstract: Acts of anti-Semitism have increased nearly 10 percent over the past year in Australia, a report by a Jewish council says.

2017-11-26.title: No Man’s Land: Barbuda After Irma
2017-11-26.abstract: The Caribbean island of Barbuda felt the full force of Hurricane Irma. Now, a struggle to control its future is underway. On one side are Barbudans who own the land and want to preserve their unique way of life — on the other, the government and foreign investors who see opportunity in disaster.

2017-11-26.title: Pakistan Deploys Military Troops After Violent Clashes in Islamabad
2017-11-26.abstract: Military and government officials met in the capital in a bid to restore law and order after deadly clashes between supporters of a firebrand cleric and the police.

2017-11-26.title: Pope Francis’ Dilemma in Myanmar: Whether to Say ‘Rohingya’
2017-11-26.abstract: The pope, who is visiting on Monday, received a plea from the nation’s cardinal not to mention the Muslim minority but focus on the group in “a way that doesn’t hurt anybody.”

2017-11-27.title: Sabbath Railway Work in Israel Shakes Netanyahu’s Coalition
2017-11-27.abstract: A resurgence of Israel’s so-called Sabbath wars leads an ultra-Orthodox minister to quit on his rabbi’s orders, putting pressure on others in Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s cabinet.

2017-11-27.title: Pakistan Strikes Deal With Islamist Protesters in Islamabad
2017-11-27.abstract: In an agreement brokered by the army, the law minister, whom protesters accused of blasphemy, resigned in return for peace on the capital’s streets.

2017-11-27.title: Bali Volcano Eruption Triggers More Evacuations
2017-11-27.abstract: Officials have expanded an exclusion zone over fears that a larger eruption could follow.

2017-11-27.title: Fontana, an Artist Ahead of His Time
2017-11-27.abstract: Lucio Fontana, who died in 1968, was best known for his slashes on canvas. An exhibition in Milan is casting a new light on a less known part of his work that sets him at the forefront of art installation.

2017-11-27.title: Prince Harry, Meghan Markle and News of a Royal Wedding
2017-11-27.abstract: The prince, a grandson of Queen Elizabeth II, and Ms. Markle, an American actress, will be married in the spring, the royal family said.

2017-11-28.title: ‘It Was a Blood Bath’: Freight Trains Kill 110 Reindeer in Norway
2017-11-28.abstract: Animal advocacy groups urge speed limits, new fencing and digital tracking to prevent more accidents.

2017-11-28.title: Prince Harry Casts Aside Ghosts of Royal Marriages Past
2017-11-28.abstract: Harry, fifth in line to the throne, may never be king. But his mere choice of bride may give Britain a strong shove into the future.

2017-11-28.title: Venezuela Appoints General to Tighten Grip on Oil Industry
2017-11-28.abstract: Skeptics said the appointment at the state-owned oil company was part of a move by the president to consolidate control over the industry.

2017-11-28.title: Mexico’s Finance Minister Says He’ll Run for President
2017-11-28.abstract: José Antonio Meade stepped down from his post and said he would run next year as the candidate of the governing Institutional Revolutionary Party.

2017-11-28.title: China Sentences Taiwanese Human Rights Activist in Subversion Case
2017-11-28.abstract: Lee Ming-cheh was sentenced to five years in prison in the first case in which China has convicted a Taiwanese citizen of state subversion.

2017-11-29.title: Euthanasia Law Passes in Australia for First Time
2017-11-29.abstract: After long debate, and despite opposition from many doctors, the state of Victoria legalized assisted dying for some terminally ill patients who request it.

2017-11-29.title: Charges Cast Spotlight on Singapore’s Strict Rules on Public Gatherings
2017-11-29.abstract: Jolovan Wham held small gatherings, including one featuring the Hong Kong protest leader Joshua Wong by video, that resulted in charges by the police.

2017-11-29.title: In China, Fears That New Anticorruption Agency Will Be Above the Law
2017-11-29.abstract: Mainstream lawyers and law professors in China are taking a risk in speaking out against a move that they say would violate the country’s Constitution.

2017-11-29.title: Philippines Killed Communist Rebels Near Manila, Military Says
2017-11-29.abstract: The fighting came on the same day that the government closed the door on peace talks.

2017-11-29.title: Croatian War Criminal Dies After Swallowing Poison in Court
2017-11-29.abstract: At a tribunal considering appeals by six Croats convicted over actions in Bosnia, a former general swallowed the contents of a small container after his sentence was upheld.

2017-11-30.title: Washington Eyes a Cold War Strategy Against North Korea
2017-11-30.abstract: A focus on nuclear deterrence would fall short of President Trump’s promises to finally “solve” the problem with North Korea.

2017-11-30.title: 29 Argentines Sentenced to Life in Prison in ‘Death Flights’ Trial
2017-11-30.abstract: Argentina’s most high-profile human rights trial included testimony about the former military dictatorship’s practice of killing civilians by throwing them out of aircraft.

2017-11-30.title: Reza Zarrab Testifies That He Bribed Turkish Minister
2017-11-30.abstract: The gold trader told jurors why he’s helping prosecutors: “Cooperation was the fastest way to accept responsibility and to get out of jail at once.”

2017-11-30.title: Political Unrest Grips Honduras After Disputed Election
2017-11-30.abstract: The opposition leader, Salvador Nasralla, accused vote counters of trying to “steal our victory” to leave President Juan Orlando Hernández in power.

2017-11-30.title: A Generation in Japan Faces a Lonely Death
2017-11-30.abstract: In postwar Japan, a single-minded focus on rapid economic growth helped erode family ties. Now, a generation of elderly Japanese is dying alone.
